input.prompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.content:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?
data.transformedContent:

We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.

input.initialPrompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.reasoningText:
The user migrated from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP) and is hitting exit code 8 when test assemblies produce zero tests. This is a known behavioral difference — MTP returns a non-zero exit code when no tests are discovered, whereas VSTest treated that as success.

Let me look at their project files to give precise guidance.

data.content:
This is a known MTP behavioral difference. **Exit code 8 = "no tests discovered"**. VSTest returned 0 for empty assemblies; MTP deliberately fails.

The fix is to tell MTP to return exit code 0 when no tests are found. Add this property to your project:

arguments.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Treat "no tests discovered" as success (exit code 0 instead of 8) -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. With MTP, when no tests are discovered (because `INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS` isn't defined), the runner returns exit code 8 ("no tests found"), which CI treats as failure. VSTest returned 0 in this case.

The fix is to set the MSBuild property `TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests` to `0` in the project file (or `Directory.Build.props`), or use the environment variable `TESTINGPLATFORM_EXITCODE_IGNORE` or the CLI option `--ignore-exit-code 8`.

The cleanest fix for this project is adding `<TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>` to the csproj.

arguments.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Treat "no tests discovered" as success (exit code 0 instead of 8) -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>

data.content:
The fix works — **exit code is now 0** even with zero tests discovered.

**What changed:** `<TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>` tells MTP to return exit code 0 (success) instead of 8 when no tests are found.

**Alternatives** if you can't change the csproj (e.g., CI-only fix):
- CLI flag: `dotnet test --ignore-exit-code 8`
- Environment variable: `TESTINGPLATFORM_EXITCODE_IGNORE=8`

If you have multiple test projects, consider putting the property in a shared `Directory.Build.props` instead.
data.reasoningText: The key result is `EXIT CODE: 0`. Even though MTP still reports the "no tests" message, the exit code is now 0 instead of 8, which means CI will pass. The fix works.
